skill.name: fal-platform
skill.description: Guidance for working with Fal platform APIs for models, pricing, and usage tracking.
skill.summary: Use Fal platform API patterns for model operations, pricing review, and usage analysis.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found two hardcoded URLs and two weak-cryptography alerts. The URLs are source repository references, and the weak-cryptography alerts are false positives on descriptive text with no cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: Documentation Repository Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22. These are public source repository references, not runtime network requests or data exfiltration paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain only GitHub repository links. No fetch call, command, script, credential, or automatic network behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20. The cited lines contain plain descriptive text about platform APIs and do not reference or invoke a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is documentation-only and has no code execution surface. The cited text has no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic usage.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear guidance when working with Fal platform APIs across model, pricing, and usage tasks. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure platform API work with focused prompts and review steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Fal platform API guidance.
Supports model management planning and review.
Frames pricing analysis tasks for platform APIs.
Frames usage tracking tasks for platform APIs.
Points users to the public source repository for more detail.
limitations: Does not include executable API client code.
Does not authenticate to Fal services or manage credentials.
Does not provide endpoint-specific request examples.
Does not validate live pricing or usage data.

use_cases.title: Plan Model Operations
use_cases.description: Create a focused plan for reviewing model management tasks before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review Pricing Questions
use_cases.description: Organize pricing API questions and identify information needed for cost review.

use_cases.title: Track Platform Usage
use_cases.description: Prepare usage tracking workflows for reporting and operational monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Identify the Right Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fal-platform skill to decide whether my task is about model management, pricing, or usage tracking. Summarize the next steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for a new platform API task.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Model Management Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fal-platform skill to outline a model management workflow for my Fal platform project. Include assumptions and missing inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Structure a Pricing Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fal-platform skill to create a pricing review checklist for Fal platform API usage. Separate required data from optional analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Design Usage Tracking Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fal-platform skill to design a usage tracking review process for an existing Fal integration. Include risks, validation steps, and reporting needs.

output_examples.input: Help me plan a Fal platform model management task.
output_examples.output: A concise workflow that separates model inventory, ownership, update checks, risk review, and follow-up questions.

output_examples.input: Review what I need before analyzing pricing.
output_examples.output: A pricing checklist covering usage scope, billing period, model selection, expected volume, and missing cost inputs.

output_examples.input: Create a usage tracking review plan.
output_examples.output: A review plan covering tracked metrics, reporting cadence, validation checks, and operational owners.

best_practices: Confirm the exact Fal platform task before requesting implementation details.
Keep credentials and billing data out of prompts unless explicitly required.
Validate live API behavior against official Fal documentation before deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this skill as a complete API client.
Do not assume pricing or usage data is current without verification.
Do not place secrets or tokens in shared prompt history.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure work around Fal platform APIs for model management, pricing, and usage tracking.

faq.question: Does it call Fal APIs directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and patterns, not executable API calls.

faq.question: Can it manage credentials?
faq.answer: No. Credential handling must be implemented separately with secure storage and access controls.

faq.question: Can it provide current pricing?
faq.answer: No. Current pricing should be verified against official Fal sources before decisions.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Developers, product managers, and platform engineers working with Fal platform API tasks can use it.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation-only, and static high-risk findings were false positives.
